content.full_text:
CITY OF HEALDSBURG
Civic Hall — Administrative Offices
P.O. Box 578
126 Matheson Street
Healdsburg, CA 95448-0578

City Manager's Office (707) 431-3317
Finance 431-3305
Parks & Recreation 431-3301
Personnel 431-3322
FAX 431-2852

January 7, 1991

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, #800
Sacramento, CA 95814

RE: REQUEST FOR LETTER OPINION

Gentlemen:

I am the Finance Director/Assistant Treasurer for the City of
Healdsburg. In that capacity I am responsible for investigating
and managing the City's idle cash and investment portfolio.

Recently I was approached by the President of Windsor Oaks
National Bank to invest city funds in bank certificates or
deposit. One of our City Councilman, Edgar Deas, is a major
investor and Director of the Bank which, in my opinion, precludes
me from investing City funds in the Windsor Bank. First, am I
correct in this opinion and secondly, would the Councilman's
relationship with the Bank also preclude other City banking
relationships with that bank?

In implementing City investments, we utilize interim transfers.
The City's depository resolution requires, dependent on the
particular transaction, two or four signatures, i.e., the City
Manager; Finance Director, Mayor or Vice Mayor. On occasion, but
not typically, the Mayor or Vice Mayor may sign such documents
thus approving such a transaction. Mr. Deas is the Vice Mayor
this year and would become the Mayor next year. While the
actions are ministerial in nature, could his participation
preclude any banking actions on my part?

Inasmuch as the Windsor Oaks Bank includes a number of area
developers among its stockholders and on its Board, it is likely
that the Bank will be the provider of a variety of security for
public or quasi-public improvements for City development
projects. Normally, the Finance Director approves or
disapproves such security. Am I constrained in any way from
accepting Windsor Oaks Bank Security by reason of Mr. Deas'
position with the Bank?

I can see this appears to be a very faint or low-contrast document image, but the text is too illegible for me to transcribe accurately. The image shows what appears to be a page with text, but the content is not clear enough to read with confidence and transcribe verbatim as requested.

To provide you with an accurate transcription following your rules, I would need a clearer, higher-contrast version of this document.

Quite aside from any legal conflict, it is my opinion that at a minimum any direct banking relationship with Windsor Oaks creates the appearance of conflict and therefore should be avoided. On the other hand, the City is essentially working with all banks in the area to focus their efforts under the Community Reinvestment Act to better serve our community. Do you have any guidelines under these circumstances?

Respectfully,

[Signature: Kurt Hahn]

KURT HAHN
Finance Director
City of Healdsburg
